mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits lower discount rates and easier financial conditions to equities, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is already near the effective lower bound, because further easing changes expected short rates and risk appetite more than inflation expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after months with declining FEDFUNDS, or if the signal flips negative in a rolling 2-year window with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates enough to force policy tightening, higher expected real rates compress equity multiples and reduce forward S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is triggered by an inflation breakout, not by stable inflation levels.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its prior 12-month median and FEDFUNDS is flat or rising
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the CPIAUCSL-breakout condition has no incremental predictive power beyond FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE reflects weakening household income and future demand, which lowers earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is most informative when unemployment is rising rather than merely high.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after unemployment jumps, or if the event study shows no worse outcomes than unconditional months.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST signals household spending resilience and housing-driven demand, which supports revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect depends on broad demand strengthening rather than one series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST growth does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered months fail to deliver positive 20-day returns relative to the unconditional sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and utilization, which increases expected corporate cash flow and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is stronger when production growth is broad-based rather than a one-month rebound.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not higher after positive INDPRO trend changes, or if the sign of the relationship is unstable across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline together, labor market strength implies rising income and consumer demand, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week as investors reprice near-term earnings. The signal is a joint labor-market confirmation, not a single-series trend.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change turns positive while UNRATE falls from its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS/UNRATE condition does not improve 5-day returns versus either series alone, or if the joint trigger yields no excess hit rate above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated FEDFUNDS level above the post-crisis low-rate regime raises the equity discount rate and signals tighter liquidity conditions, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is strongest when rates are high relative to the recent low-rate environment, not merely moving sideways.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes do not correspond to negative 5-day forward returns, or if the conditional mean return is indistinguishable from zero after accounting for the level threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS marks broad demand softening, which lowers revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails if output weakens without a matching sales slowdown, because then the demand channel is not confirmed.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not predict worse 20-day returns than either series alone, or if negative-return frequency is not elevated above 55% in triggered windows.
